title: Pediatric vitamin D review

investigations.text: Supports documenting why an existing vitamin D test was clinically obtained and whether symptoms or high-risk context were assessed; it does not interpret the result or generate testing.
investigations.rationale: Supports documenting why an existing test was obtained for paediatric vitamin D review; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting why an existing vitamin D test was clinically obtained and whether symptoms or high-risk context were assessed; it does not interpret the result or generate testing.

source.title: Vitamin D: supplement use in specific population groups

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 7 — only test when symptomatic, very high risk, or clinically indicated

medication.text: Supports clinician documentation of already reported vitamin D supplement use in qualified encounters without generating product, dose or duration.
medication.rationale: Supports recording already reported supplement use for paediatric vitamin D review; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports clinician documentation of already reported vitamin D supplement use in qualified encounters without generating product, dose or duration.

follow_up.text: Supports clinician recording of age, pregnancy or breastfeeding, low sun exposure, covering skin, darker skin and other entered risk context without diagnosing deficiency.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Age, indication, symptoms, risks, exact result and units, interpretation, product, dose, adherence, safety review and clinician plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for paediatric vitamin D review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
